title: Rothia hirsuta
taxonomy: Rothia hirsuta belongs to the family Cactaceae and is native to Mexico. It is a species of cactus. Further classification includes its genus, which is part of the tribe Cacteae. Rothia hirsuta is a type of cactus characterized by its distinctive hairy stems. It is a relatively rare and unique species within the cactus family.

intro: Rothia hirsuta, also known as Hairy Rothia, is a plant species in the family Capparaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rothia hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
